a strange meadow@wowperfect1/9/2023, 5:52 PM

fun fact about barcode scanners!

when you plug a usb barcode scanner into your computer, what kind of device do u think it tells the computer it is?

a keyboard. barcode scanners literally just say they're keyboards and then send the keystrokes for whatever you scan with them lmao

bark. bark bark@bark1/9/2023, 9:23 PM

plus! as far as i know [though times may have a changed] a lot of barcode scanners are configured using special barcodes. bleep you're now in a new mode

alyx@alyx1/9/2023, 10:04 PM

somewhere i have one from the early 2000s that presents itself as a serial device and you have to do special things to capture input from serial

in reply to @bark's post:

Hen Arcade@Hen1/10/2023, 5:40 PM

Oh hey! Something from the industry I work in! Fun fact about those special new mode barcodes:

Some devices will come with a whole-ass laminated binder that contains hundreds if not thousands of pre-configured modes, and special programming modes where you can scan multiple barcode modes to set up a scanner device to work in particular ways, and then an "end programming" barcode to close it up.

Need to append a special character than a carriage return? There's a barcode mode for that. Need return codes to always scan in camel case? Mode for that too!
